clean_body: We're sharing the stories of two women with great ideas. And we're also bringing you fun ways to make the summer break great again! If you are having trouble viewing this email click here to view in your browser. [] [] [] May 27, 2025 • Newsletter Women with Bright Ideas Moms for America Weekly Podcast [] [] Moms for America Weekly Podcast [] At CPAC this year, Moms for America talked to so many amazing women making a difference and forging their own paths. In today's episode, we are sharing two of those inspiring conversations. Comedian Karith Foster knows firsthand the pitfalls of DEI and "woke" policies. She is bringing a commonsense approach to conversations about our country and race. Amy Robbins is a mom who wanted safety-focused athletic wear that was also comfortable and fashionable. While training for a marathon, she discovered there were no athletic clothes that allow women to concealed carry. So, she made Alexo Athletica that does just that. Like, subscribe and share. View the Podcast [] Listen to the Podcast [] Listen to the Podcast on Spotify [] Moms for America Weekly Blog [] [] Creative Ideas Moms Can Use to Make Summer Great Again Summer is almost here. The kids are finishing the school year and gearing up for their long-awaited break. Whether your budget for this year’s summer fun is tiny or tremendous, we know moms are the experts at creating sun-splashed adventures and lasting memories. You don’t have to plan a grand or pricey vacation to discover the fun nearby – even in your own backyard. This week, we’re sharing some ideas to help you make the season shine! Read the Blog [] [] In Truth & Liberty, Kimberly Fletcher Founder and President Moms for America® The Mom View [] [] Empowering Conversations Start Here! From parenting to public policy, The MomView delves into the topics that shape our culture, impact our nation and influence our homes, because every issue is a mom issue. Season 2 Episodes on Mondays at 10am ET View on YouTube [] View on Rumble [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] Copyright © 2025 Moms for America, All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: Moms for America 1440 State HWY 248, Suite Q304 Branson, MO 65616 Visit our Website [] Visit our Store [] Visit us on Truth Social [] DONATE TODAY []

name: VAP Research Team
subject: Tracking poll: Seizing voting machines?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=oar2hxVfJssUFUKXdMKGLa3SPlpBQfs7BGoL0889w8kNfUb8OZ9xTwEEPjSeQI1S8BuQOF7HY7ajS4uWLz4dO4o896JPgPU/n21neJy5NlQ=]
@NorahODonnell: The January 6th committee shows a draft executive order from Dec. 2020 that would direct the Secretary of Defense to seize voting machines immediately. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/NOD_SeizeVotingMachines.png]
The January 6th committee showed evidence in a hearing that President Trump was going to order the Department of Defense to seize voting machines around the country to search for evidence of wrongdoing.
While the order was never given, this highlights the lengths some people were willing to go to justify their fantasies about elections they see as "stolen".
Team : We're looking to hear from voters in your area about your thoughts on this. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Should the President have the power to order the military to seize voting machines without proof of wrongdoing?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=oar2hxVfJssUFUKXdMKGLa3SPlpBQfs7BGoL0889w8kNfUb8OZ9xTwEEPjSeQI1S8BuQOF7HY7ajS4uWLz4dO4o896JPgPU/n21neJy5NlQ=] NO [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=oar2hxVfJssUFUKXdMKGLa3SPlpBQfs7BGoL0889w8kNfUb8OZ9xTwEEPjSeQI1S8BuQOF7HY7ajS4uWLz4dO4o896JPgPU/n21neJy5NlQ=]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=oar2hxVfJssUFUKXdMKGLa3SPlpBQfs7BGoL0889w8kNfUb8OZ9xTwEEPjSeQI1S8BuQOF7HY7ajS4uWLz4dO4o896JPgPU/n21neJy5NlQ=]

## Constitution Doesn’t Guarantee Birthright Citizenship
President Donald Trump signed an executive order to end birthright citizenship on his first day back in office in January. subject: Time for a Sacred Pause
body:
America needs more than cookouts on Memorial Day--we need a collective reckoning with where we're at.

| |

# [Time for a Sacred Pause](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164567307&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0NTY3MzA3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDgzNTk0MjIsImV4cCI6MTc1MDk1MTQyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4d-D074GoA9cyIDHyGG_MpqKufgfxV6m60vCoaVVBlA)
### America needs more than cookouts on Memorial Day--we need a collective reckoning with where we're at.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| May 27
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164567307&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0NTY3MzA3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0ODM1OTQyMiwiZXhwIjoxNzUwOTUxNDIyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.hoDmI3LwBUEbyzSVIrgFGVzvfS8WYEiYxd7p74Xsezk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164567307&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0NTY3MzA3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDgzNTk0MjIsImV4cCI6MTc1MDk1MTQyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4d-D074GoA9cyIDHyGG_MpqKufgfxV6m60vCoaVVBlA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164567307&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0NTY3MzA3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDgzNTk0MjIsImV4cCI6MTc1MDk1MTQyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4d-D074GoA9cyIDHyGG_MpqKufgfxV6m60vCoaVVBlA)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcGF0cmlja211cnBoeXBhL3AvdGltZS1mb3ItYS1zYWNyZWQtcGF1c2U_.55UBv8xJCTD4936GwjqgMbdXSZxkSQGs2xqIbd0wozQ?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/time-for-a-sacred-pause?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

For most Americans, Memorial Day is synonymous with sunshine, barbecues, and the start of summer. Stores hold sales, pools reopen, and we ease into vacation mode. But the deeper meaning of Memorial Day is far less comfortable. It demands something more from all of us than just another three-day weekend. It demands that we look directly at the human price we pay for our way of life.
We've become a nation adrift, disconnected from service, and ultimately, from one another.
Since the founding of our nation, [over one million service members have died](https://substack.com/redirect/cd355c73-8114-48cb-a7a0-af61130aa2c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) \- each one of these was a living testimony of doing something bigger than themselves - laying their lives on the line for their brothers and sisters, and for our nation.
Having worn the uniform in combat myself, I understand the meaning of the holiday. I served in the company of heroes. In 2004, I deployed to Baghdad with the 82nd Airborne. Nineteen of my fellow paratroopers didn't make it home. They are not names on a wall to me. They are faces I can't forget. They gave the ultimate sacrifice, these aren't abstract figures--they're real faces of friends and patriots we've lost. Faces I think about not just on Memorial Day--they sometimes haunt me in my darkest moments when survivor's guilt creeps in. I'm often reminded of the weight of their absence and of our responsibility to live up to their sacrifice.
There's a scene at the end of _[Saving Private Ryan](https://substack.com/redirect/469f3c92-b9ac-4684-a37d-ab3ec28f377e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ that still wrecks me no matter how many times I watch it. Matt Damon's character, as an old man, returns to Normandy with his wife, children, and grandchildren. He stands at the grave of Captain John Miller, the officer who died saving him, and begins to break down. In a moving monologue, he says,"I tried to live my life the best I could and hope I earned all of what you've done for me." Choked up, he then turns to his wife and pleads, "Tell me I've led a good life. Tell me I'm a good man."
That moment captures exactly what Memorial Day should feel like. An absolute gut punch for the people who served and sacrificed their lives for our freedom.
Thanks for reading, this post is public so feel free to share it and spread the word.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=164567307&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY0NTY3MzA3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDgzNTk0MjIsImV4cCI6MTc1MDk1MTQyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4d-D074GoA9cyIDHyGG_MpqKufgfxV6m60vCoaVVBlA)
President John F. Kennedy--himself a decorated World War II Navy veteran--issued a Memorial Day proclamation in 1961 that still deserves our attention:
> _" Now, Therefore, I, John F. Kennedy, President of the United States, do hereby urge the people of the United States to observe Tuesday, May 30, 1961, Memorial Day, by invoking the blessing of God on those who have died in defense of our country, and by praying for a new world of law where peace and justice shall prevail and a life of opportunity shall be assured for all... I also urge the press, radio, television, and all other media of information to cooperate in this observance." \- President John F. Kennedy _
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a15a7ef8-bc85-4764-a97b-b7905cce4ddd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
President Kennedy laying a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, 1963. via Flickr Images
Compare that level of reverence with how many television or radio stations paused yesterday at 11 am. What do you say to your sons and daughters, or nieces and nephews, who express a desire to serve? Too often, we treat military service as something for "other people's kids." When 78% of troops come from military families, we're basically only asking a "warrior class" in America to answer the call to serve.
The Greek historian Thucydides once warned, "The nation that makes a great distinction between its scholars and its warriors will have its thinking done by cowards and its fighting done by fools."
America at her best is a blend of Athens and Sparta--a nation of reluctant warriors, courageous thinkers, and engaged citizens. A nation that strives to be better in our classrooms, communities and across the country.
If we truly honor those who've fallen, we must also support the families they left behind. According to the [Military Times](https://substack.com/redirect/5e245539-ee70-4bf1-a272-0728d8d0d2cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), since 9/11, more than 7,000 American service members have been killed in Iraq and Afghanistan, the longest wars in American history. We must also honor those who return home carrying invisible wounds of war. Veterans face higher rates of PTSD, depression, and suicide compared to the civilian population. Since 2001, [more than ](https://substack.com/redirect/bbf26e3b-9d06-4442-8816-ec265ebb6d2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[120,000 veterans](https://substack.com/redirect/bbf26e3b-9d06-4442-8816-ec265ebb6d2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ have died by suicide](https://substack.com/redirect/bbf26e3b-9d06-4442-8816-ec265ebb6d2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), making it the second leading cause of death among post-9/11 veterans. Today, veterans face a suicide rate that is **57% higher than the national average**.. This crisis means that on average, [we lose ](https://substack.com/redirect/495a162f-e184-4d6c-8333-f59467b5f27e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[17 veterans every single day](https://substack.com/redirect/495a162f-e184-4d6c-8333-f59467b5f27e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ to suicide](https://substack.com/redirect/495a162f-e184-4d6c-8333-f59467b5f27e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d8d1e16c-8af7-4c56-835c-67229811e2d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Via wefacethefight.org
This is a national emergency.
You can help make a difference by joining [www.wefacethefight.org](https://substack.com/redirect/50bda45c-0729-4583-bf2c-ba6ff4c8133e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to combat veteran suicide. I proudly serve as their National Honorary Chairman.
The grief of war is generational. It reaches back to Normandy, to Vietnam, to Korea.
In a time of conspiracy theories, division, and outrage, honoring our fallen means more than remembrance. It means living lives worthy of their sacrifice. Living lives rooted in truth, unity, and the kind of compassion that builds a better country.
Memorial Day asks us to uphold the freedoms we often take for granted, and to ensure that our veterans' deaths were not in vain. That we "earn it" every single day. It's a day that should cause us to pause, reflect, and recommit ourselves to building a country worthy of their sacrifice.
So, this summer, while you're enjoying your days off with friends and family, take a moment to honor the memories of our fallen and all they stood for. Ask yourself whether you are part of the solution in making our nation a "more perfect union." Are you living a life worthy of the freedom you enjoy?
--Patrick J. Murphy
_The Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a Wharton lecturer, Vetrepreneur, and the 32nd Army Under Secretary after earning the Bronze Star for service in Baghdad, Iraq as an All-American with the 82nd Airborne Division --@PatrickMurphyPA on Instagram and Twitter._
Thanks for reading The Secretary's SitRep. Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wYXRyaWNrbXVycGh5cGEuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxNjQ1NjczMDcsInMiOjQzOTkxMzMsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDgzNTk0MjIsImV4cCI6MTc1MDk1MTQyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.FWE7IfdPZQ-QLrfRNfpoLkuMy7Pu4Qn25d5vvC0b6ao?)
\- _**Honorable Patrick J. Murphy**_

* **TOP NEWS**

"**CenterPoint saw 167,000 power outages in Houston area after severe storms** ," Houston Chronicle's**Caroline Wilburn** \-- "The Houston area saw power outages, with the most widespread outages to the north, and downed trees [as severe thunderstorms moved across the region](https://substack.com/redirect/39f1c6c4-cd9c-4ddd-92bc-20b14d062a2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) early Tuesday.

Nearly 100,000 customers experienced power outages around 6:30 a.m. Tuesday, with CenterPoint Energy [reporting 1,063 outages](https://substack.com/redirect/7a3ca1e0-1912-4722-af43-83c090099000?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) affecting 83,488 customers and Entergy [reporting 11,000 without power](https://substack.com/redirect/6e4760f7-e736-4a12-8478-0aa0f63e89a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Montgomery County.

The outages had dropped to around 66,000 for CenterPoint and around 9,000 for Entergy as of 9:20 a.m.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/5beb500e-9f5e-4692-8295-fb5428de2d34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Montgomery County Judge **Mark Keough** said he spoke with power companies Tuesday morning as crews were already working to restore power across the county.

"Last night's storm was similar to a Category 1 hurricane blowing through the county," Keough wrote on Facebook. "Thankfully the power outages are not as large as what we experience during a hurricane and for the most part should be online sometime today."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/5beb500e-9f5e-4692-8295-fb5428de2d34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Thunderstorms also brought heavy winds and some damage. A home in northwest Harris County was damaged when a large tree fell onto the roof, Precinct 4 Constable **Mark Herman** said on social media.

Multiple fire crews battled a blaze in the attic of a Woodlands home in the 2000 block of Bennet Lane after it was apparently struck by lightning, according to The Woodlands Fire Department. No one was injured in the fire, according to officials." * **TOP NEWS**

"**CenterPoint saw 167,000 power outages in Houston area after severe storms** ," Houston Chronicle's**Caroline Wilburn** \-- "The Houston area saw power outages, with the most widespread outages to the north, and downed trees [as severe thunderstorms moved across the region](https://substack.com/redirect/460f54ce-c4fa-48c3-8d12-c3073b4e958b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) early Tuesday.

Nearly 100,000 customers experienced power outages around 6:30 a.m. Tuesday, with CenterPoint Energy [reporting 1,063 outages](https://substack.com/redirect/cffe8cbd-80b3-4349-96b6-21f15382ed68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) affecting 83,488 customers and Entergy [reporting 11,000 without power](https://substack.com/redirect/f92928e4-bb56-411a-be27-8f72a6339dee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Montgomery County.

The outages had dropped to around 66,000 for CenterPoint and around 9,000 for Entergy as of 9:20 a.m.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/b50fa72a-2838-421f-b5b0-a74fbe8de857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Montgomery County Judge **Mark Keough** said he spoke with power companies Tuesday morning as crews were already working to restore power across the county.

"Last night's storm was similar to a Category 1 hurricane blowing through the county," Keough wrote on Facebook. "Thankfully the power outages are not as large as what we experience during a hurricane and for the most part should be online sometime today."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/b50fa72a-2838-421f-b5b0-a74fbe8de857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Thunderstorms also brought heavy winds and some damage. A home in northwest Harris County was damaged when a large tree fell onto the roof, Precinct 4 Constable Mark Herman said on social media.

Multiple fire crews battled a blaze in the attic of a Woodlands home in the 2000 block of Bennet Lane after it was apparently struck by lightning, according to The Woodlands Fire Department. No one was injured in the fire, according to officials." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/a87a605d-faba-4582-b126-5a25f2f28e59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS "CenterPoint saw 167,000 power outages in Houston area after severe storms ," Houston Chronicle'sCaroline Wilburn \-- "The Houston area saw power outages, with the most widespread outages to the north, and downed trees early Tuesday. Nearly 100,000 customers experienced power outages around 6:30 a.m. Tuesday, with CenterPoint Energy affecting 83,488 customers and Entergy in Montgomery County. The outages had dropped to around 66,000 for CenterPoint and around 9,000 for Entergy as of 9:20 a.m. Montgomery County Judge Mark Keough said he spoke with power companies Tuesday morning as crews were already working to restore power across the county. "Last night's storm was similar to a Category 1 hurricane blowing through the county," Keough wrote on Facebook. "Thankfully the power outages are not as large as what we experience during a hurricane and for the most part should be online sometime today." Thunderstorms also brought heavy winds and some damage. A home in northwest Harris County was damaged when a large tree fell onto the roof, Precinct 4 Constable Mark Herman said on social media. Multiple fire crews battled a blaze in the attic of a Woodlands home in the 2000 block of Bennet Lane after it was apparently struck by lightning, according to The Woodlands Fire Department. No one was injured in the fire, according to officials." Nearly 100,000 customers experienced power outages around 6:30 a.m. Tuesday, with CenterPoint Energy affecting 83,488 customers and Entergy in Montgomery County. The outages had dropped to around 66,000 for CenterPoint and around 9,000 for Entergy as of 9:20 a.m. » Montgomery County Judge Mark Keough said he spoke with power companies Tuesday morning as crews were already working to restore power across the county. "Last night's storm was similar to a Category 1 hurricane blowing through the county," Keough wrote on Facebook. "Thankfully the power outages are not as large as what we experience during a hurricane and for the most part should be online sometime today." » Thunderstorms also brought heavy winds and some damage. A home in northwest Harris County was damaged when a large tree fell onto the roof, Precinct 4 Constable Mark Herman said on social media. Multiple fire crews battled a blaze in the attic of a Woodlands home in the 2000 block of Bennet Lane after it was apparently struck by lightning, according to The Woodlands Fire Department. No one was injured in the fire, according to officials." body:

Peter,

I have some big news to share: We just took a major step to protect children and YOUR tax dollars.

As part of the “big, beautiful bill” that just passed the House, I led the charge to defund taxpayer-funded gender transition procedures for adults and minors – a move that will save Americans approximately $800 million.

This isn't just about fiscal responsibility; it's about safeguarding Americans from irreversible procedures that lack real scientific backing.

But the radical Left is furious.

They've already begun mobilizing, vowing to retaliate against me and other conservatives who dare to stand up for common sense.

That's why I'm reaching out to you today. I need your support to continue this fight.

[So please make a contribution that will help me counter the Left's attacks and fight back as they try to flip our Republican House majority.](https://secure.winred.com/crenshawforcongress/crenshaw-amendment-/?utm_campaign=20250522&utm_medium=email&utm_source=TLC_A)

name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: Cautionary Tale of Gender-Affirming Care
body:
Beth Ann Rosica | B+L Featured Columnist | | | |
---
| | |
---
| **__Beth Ann Rosica_ : CHOP or politics? Hospital’s loyalty to Levine and WPATH tops medical caution on child transitions_**
---
| [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=cbd7e2711c&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
* * *
---
| ''Earlier this month, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) issued a [report](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a8e39a79e0&e=b7b3eddce2) on pediatric gender dysphoria and concluded there is little to no evidence to support “gender-affirming care” — interventions designed to support children and adolescents who believe their gender identity differs from their biology.

This report is strikingly similar to the [Cass Review](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=fccb47968f&e=b7b3eddce2) which was commissioned by the National Health Service in England and published a little over a year ago. As a result of that publication, many European countries stopped or significantly limited these services for children..."
---

---
|
---
|
## About Beth Ann Rosica:
---
| | [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c9b328e96d&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
Beth Ann Rosica holds a Ph.D. in Education and has dedicated her career to advocating on behalf of at-risk children and families. She became politically active during the extended school closures, speaking out about the detrimental effects on children. Beth Ann led the local grassroots movement to open schools and eliminate mandatory masks while assisting school board candidates across the state and country with their campaigns. She has a consulting business and covers education issues for _Broad + Liberty_. Beth Ann resides in West Chester, PA with her husband and two teenage sons.

Read more from Beth Ann [here](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=372a867a94&e=b7b3eddce2) at _Broad + Liberty_.

Beth Ann led the local grassroots movement to open schools and eliminate mandatory masks while assisting school board candidates across the state and country with their campaigns. She has a consulting business and covers education issues for Broad + Liberty. Beth Ann resides in West Chester, PA with her husband and two teenage sons. Read more from Beth Ann at Broad + Liberty. | | | We at are proud to provide readers across our region, state and beyond, with insightful, original investigative reports, and thought-leadership, on education, crime, business and government. name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: DNC People’s Town Hall in MI-07
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2284%2E135344%2Er0OI0y)

Last week, House Republicans passed President Trump's "Big Beautiful Bill" in the dark of night. **The entire premise of this bill is to cut people out of Medicaid to pay for tax cuts for some of the wealthiest Americans. That's just the math.** In Michigan, that means up to 300,000 people could lose their primary insurance, and it will jack up the price of healthcare for *all* of us.
We need every American to hear those facts. That's why I'm excited to partner with Democratic National Committee for their next People's Town Hall, this time in Michigan's 7th Congressional District. The event will take place Friday, June 6th in Lansing.
**Please sign-up to attend the event here:[michigandems.com/CD7TownHall**](http://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3770?t=2&akid=2284%2E135344%2Er0OI0y)
I’m also excited to be joined by my friend, Representative Kristen McDonald Rivet. We will take your questions, and have an honest conversation about the way forward.
[](http://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3770?t=3&akid=2284%2E135344%2Er0OI0y)
Looking forward to seeing you there!
Friend, if you are unable to attend, [**please consider making a contribution to support my work and help push back against this bill.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-may25.BfDGgM&refcode=em250527-1&t=4&akid=2284%2E135344%2Er0OI0y)
Elissa
* * *

👋🏻 **Wait, before you click away!** 🇺🇸
United States Senators often keep on fundraising after their election ends, but Elissa doesn't want to keep bombarding your inbox with fundraising requests.
What we will do is send you good, timely content – like this message. Stuff you might open without dread.
But she does hope you may consider making a small, monthly recurring contribution. Even just $5 or $10 – so we don't have to send those mass fundraising emails while Elissa is focused on her work as a Senator.
[**Make a recurring contribution to Elissa Slotkin's campaign here.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/1960?c0=ebs-em-may25.1ew0E2&refcode=em250527-1&t=5&akid=2284%2E135344%2Er0OI0y)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your recurring contribution instantly:_
| | **[DONATE $10 MONTHLY](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3243?c0=ebs-em-may25.brpIrP&refcode=em250527-1&t=6&akid=2284%2E135344%2Er0OI0y)**

name: Sarah McAbee, Stand in the Gap
subject: I just got off the phone with a J6er
body:
I just got off the phone with a J6er who’s struggling to return to some semblance of a normal life.

| | | [View this email in your browser](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zu58950rcq2jg68t26lf37jf6vo72uvh0jb58aqg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmap34c1h2bs)
---
| | [](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zsche8o0m9fme3f6a1p40p8qhkke68k435t0me90_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmap34c1h2bs) Friend,

I just got off the phone with a J6er who’s struggling to return to some semblance of a normal life.
_I told him I would do everything in my power to ensure he has the resources he needs to heal — physically, emotionally, and financially._
Unfortunately, this is a conversation I’ve had a lot since the pardons.

For pardoned J6ers, navigating life after being incarcerated involves numerous challenges, such as finding a job, a place to live, obtaining legal assistance, and even finding a sense of community.

**The solution is to give J6ers the support they need.**

Stand in the Gap understands the unique challenges these individuals face.

We also understand how even a little support can make a big difference.

What started as an effort to reunite families with loved ones incarcerated due to J6 has grown into a full-fledged nonprofit aimed at helping newly released J6ers navigate the challenging process of re-entry into society after being incarcerated for months and even years.

I set a goal to raise $20,000 to help us rebuild lives and offer J6ers the support, community, and resources they need to succeed.

**But we can’t do it without your generosity.**

[Please chip in whatever you can generously afford today to help us provide help, hope, and healing to these American patriots in need!](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zf4a06krg1v2l612f920qs0kccc31ha6u6hpcvgo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmap34c1h2bs)
[Donate](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zch5nkorshfqkthhiv1lo72gqi5c0jfocqpunp48_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmap34c1h2bs)
There are nearly 1,600 Americans who need to rebuild their lives.

Each one I talk to tells me how there are surprisingly few resources for people who are released from prison, especially if they are conservative or America First activists.

_For some, it’s finding a place to call home...
For others, it’s finding employment without the fear of past judgments...
For many, it’s finding emotional and spiritual healing after overcoming their ordeal._

**But the one thing that everyone of them needs, Friend,****is a sense of community – the feeling that someone has their back.**

By donating today, you can help us ensure that every J6er receives the help they need to heal mentally, emotionally, and financially. [Please consider donating $10, $35, $50, $100, or any amount you can generously afford to help us raise $20,000 before the end of the month. >>](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zphentdjbn78nijgqrfeflblddsomr708r1uvi80_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmap34c1h2bs)
[Donate](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zngg536agv9pnevg788eute8bsb5kbg5f26ml8oo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmap34c1h2bs)
Sincerely,

Sarah McAbee
Co-Founder & Chief Operating Officer, Stand in the Gap

Stand In The Gap is registered as a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. All contributions are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law. Stand In The Gap’s tax identification number is 93-1417599.

name: Lindsey Graham
subject: This is my story.
body:
Fellow American, I grew up in a single room behind my parents’ family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina. I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. |
---
Fellow American,

I grew up in a single room behind my parents' family-owned liquor store, restaurant, and pool hall in Central, South Carolina.

I was the first in my family to attend college, and while I was in school, I lost my mother and father within two years of one another. I quickly took on the responsibility of helping raise my 13-year-old sister, Darline, and running the family businesses.

[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=36593cec53ea4de1a2310db19db9482c&_e=IvqPuxJeUvhprj4L06xkaEix-79lQ3RM7wVIj0LLV_2nWxCfHN6wpGI-knI8DwKHo24DjSe_4bLy0FBbEKxahg9WXJ7lKTu3tZS9PpZs_UczxS9I1HazVnRbSC5V_SqO1SJG9OqAWt-KSYxHMaBCk_Ou1i4CeZE7NaIFbZv_XUKPVgCTuOQJKVgo2fQ3tt0E5B1r7m_Q2Xuh5FMMLJuIhWAElQU3HvAZfACZR5-_F4ydzyka4u4AzBPJBTdOx49HVi8olqSYu93ptzCi4lMTdnTjkuEr86ixgQyfA6iQnAnUsZcQS8sxnbWaGYX85Z4e88eeKWDS3sG2BxzPKC_LzQw4IrMYSlwLaHZDqeTSnwLdMGjJ-AraptUtxS067EGn)
**I had to grow up fast, Fellow American,_and I learned many life lessons at an early age._**

After attending law school, I joined the U.S. Air Force as a member of the Judge Advocate General Corps, where I served as a military prosecutor and defense attorney.

Even after being elected to the United States Senate, I continued to wear the uniform and served in the U.S. Air Force Reserves, where I performed several short deployments in the war zones of Afghanistan and Iraq.

[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=36593cec53ea4de1a2310db19db9482c&_e=IvqPuxJeUvhprj4L06xkaEix-79lQ3RM7wVIj0LLV_2nWxCfHN6wpGI-knI8DwKHo24DjSe_4bLy0FBbEKxahg9WXJ7lKTu3tZS9PpZs_UczxS9I1HazVnRbSC5V_SqO1SJG9OqAWt-KSYxHMaBCk_Ou1i4CeZE7NaIFbZv_XUKPVgCTuOQJKVgo2fQ3tt0E5B1r7m_Q2Xuh5FMMLJuIhWAElQU3HvAZfACZR5-_F4zbzXYTDkm8VSrwPAVcUsv2fP4pvJncjOTMDJzXw54WoUj2MJbeoP5GmcUDme1wN7F5U_tz94tZ5nJFb6jrllemlJdQafUWNTJ71SHY3ENXYbp-KdoYbKOSMDzicyWp5gycoKp2JAP6uLxCL9jgd7ds)
Fellow American, my story is similar to that of most Americans. I didn't grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth with everything handed to me. I had to grow up fast and work hard. I learned a lot of valuable lessons at a young age and took on a whole lot of responsibility.

But one thing stuck with me over the years -- the perspectives I gained from listening to the hard-working South Carolinians who were regulars at my family's pool hall or my brave colleagues in the military. Their perspectives, whether I agreed with them or not, helped build my foundation for understanding what Americans need.

**It's no secret that our nation is sharply divided right now, but there are some things we should all agree on.**

**We need a country that is a safe place to live and raise a family, and we need a country where the American Dream is alive and possible for everyone, no matter where they start from.**

Fellow American, I'm fighting every day to make those two things a reality. Sadly, there are people across the aisle in Washington who simply can't agree with us on these issues. They are okay with open borders and the flow of violent crime into our neighborhoods and onto our streets. They are okay with punishing hard-working Americans with higher taxes, deepening the burden on them and their families.

**[That's why I'm reaching out with a simple request: if you have a few dollars to spare, would you consider making a contribution to support my campaign by clicking this link or the button below?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=36593cec53ea4de1a2310db19db9482c&_e=IvqPuxJeUvhprj4L06xkaEix-79lQ3RM7wVIj0LLV_2nWxCfHN6wpGI-knI8DwKHo24DjSe_4bLy0FBbEKxahg9WXJ7lKTu3tZS9PpZs_UczxS9I1HazVnRbSC5V_SqO1SJG9OqAWt-KSYxHMaBCk_Ou1i4CeZE7NaIFbZv_XUKPVgCTuOQJKVgo2fQ3tt0E5B1r7m_Q2Xuh5FMMLJuIhWAElQU3HvAZfACZR5-_F4zDDTQFR6xZX7BRWfKbvCSNilqO0c_DeMh1OxHqC-aXFSCp2WqZiDM3K853q-hUf97g1mrFbhljssc1iEgjGNxUIzWA68uNguPw36HekAjmhn1iRolMtqUuJvdTU_6c0td7kzqCHJmfW9FPyjDYBIOg)**

| [** CONTRIBUTE HERE **](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=36593cec53ea4de1a2310db19db9482c&_e=IvqPuxJeUvhprj4L06xkaEix-79lQ3RM7wVIj0LLV_2nWxCfHN6wpGI-knI8DwKHo24DjSe_4bLy0FBbEKxahg9WXJ7lKTu3tZS9PpZs_UczxS9I1HazVnRbSC5V_SqO1SJG9OqAWt-KSYxHMaBCk_Ou1i4CeZE7NaIFbZv_XUKPVgCTuOQJKVgo2fQ3tt0E5B1r7m_Q2Xuh5FMMLJuIhWAElQU3HvAZfACZR5-_F4xogK5wZijMdn-1Q0NYxRi-w6KuWpuoeYsHspFxyDrLUzIrHdej6RBa2xA1ubJDi37sCFEBc5yNfu4A_ezakIhSpyn7KDU8KoEXx5Shwo7u-muzEXWzIvhP93BWRHcKWM2Q2pNvab5ejxv2AuQiZed0)
---

If you're unable to contribute financially, I understand. I'd greatly appreciate it if you could forward this email along to a friend or family member to help amplify my message.

Together, I know we can build the country that protects our communities, rewards hard work, and ensures every hard-working American has a shot at their dreams, just like I was so fortunate to have.

Thank you for taking a moment out of your busy day to read this message.

Sincerely,
Lindsey Graham

[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=36593cec53ea4de1a2310db19db9482c&_e=IvqPuxJeUvhprj4L06xkaItKrnZlvZLDAfnHXhHdk6k9H0kIrb5Yr5XsjrBG03DhcDh7E_UCtVWoyGeJwSguKPI9qlwqcItBSHp96EBLlq6zff0ZVEfoAKirSXyw6xnlDCl2d5ZjNp31v8jJukHdS4rR2o5xj-I8PORQ2NwjEfgCc-8b2S2LTP3_aJm0Z1YMgOLWu4YIt3saAPO7HRKBBz9jxtFARFEnX-OEJp9u9RA%3D)

[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=36593cec53ea4de1a2310db19db9482c&_e=IvqPuxJeUvhprj4L06xkaEix-79lQ3RM7wVIj0LLV_2nWxCfHN6wpGI-knI8DwKHo24DjSe_4bLy0FBbEKxahg9WXJ7lKTu3tZS9PpZs_UczxS9I1HazVnRbSC5V_SqO1SJG9OqAWt-KSYxHMaBCk_Ou1i4CeZE7NaIFbZv_XUKPVgCTuOQJKVgo2fQ3tt0E5B1r7m_Q2Xuh5FMMLJuIhWAElQU3HvAZfACZR5-_F4wtjOlt3YeoThJT-BGAv3Z5YfJIBROxIqjXZTbbeUH6iiAH4R4jxIOo8pzfPoBpfHZJg3i7LO4OsXY9FvIchUVpmcUkC9a7Nz4VyD_hqSFSEmEilOpLDgCFspoBJSd1FniTE3h6hW068CYPQt9Dd6g6)
In the United States Senate, South Carolina's Lindsey Graham has earned a reputation as a conservative, a problem-solver, and a leader who gets things done. He is a great friend to our men and women serving in uniform, a respected voice on national security matters, a leading advocate for cutting the out-of-control spending in Washington, and a champion for confirming conservative judges.

| [** SUPPORT SENATOR GRAHAM **](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=36593cec53ea4de1a2310db19db9482c&_e=IvqPuxJeUvhprj4L06xkaEix-79lQ3RM7wVIj0LLV_2nWxCfHN6wpGI-knI8DwKHo24DjSe_4bLy0FBbEKxahg9WXJ7lKTu3tZS9PpZs_UczxS9I1HazVnRbSC5V_SqO1SJG9OqAWt-KSYxHMaBCk_Ou1i4CeZE7NaIFbZv_XUKPVgCTuOQJKVgo2fQ3tt0E5B1r7m_Q2Xuh5FMMLJuIhWAElQU3HvAZfACZR5-_F4yxRbM1rDQlXoDP0b1l96awJfFm4wZu_t4160ehvwqJAERguZVL11wFz3iVw0YQlCPtZL488suVvxZwKTW45c06ZxW773_emfXn1PSPSp3U1eqxPaGFJIwmfrZWRIMHtn63jWomPgkm6IFsGkZC8Yls)

name: Keith
subject: We just blocked Trump—again
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417
body:
Peter,

I have some good news to share: We won a court order to stop President Trump from dismantling the Department of Education!

Thanks to the order our coalition of 20 Attorneys General secured, Trump has to halt this illegal attack on our schools until the lawsuit is settled—and reinstate all the Department of Education employees who were fired.

Tens of millions of kids across the country depend on the Department of Education. Gutting it would throw many schools and families into chaos.

We’ve created a setback for Trump and his MAGA allies, but the fight isn’t over.

Trump will keep trying to bulldoze the rule of law in order to implement his reckless, unconstitutional agenda. To be ready to stand up and stop him, we have to pick up the pace to meet our end of month fundraising goal.

Will you chip in $10, $25, $50, or whatever works for you today to help us achieve our next goal and keep reining in Trump in and his lawless actions?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
CHIP IN $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250527ke?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
CHIP IN $25 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250527ke?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
CHIP IN $75 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250527ke?express_lane=true&amount=75&refcode=express_lane_75]
CHIP IN $150 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250527ke?express_lane=true&amount=150&refcode=express_lane_150]
CHIP IN $250 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250527ke?express_lane=true&amount=250&refcode=express_lane_250]
OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250527ke?refcode=other]

Thanks so much for standing with me,

Keith Ellison

Minnesota's Political Contribution Refund (PCR) program reset on January 1. You can now use your 2025 PCR to contribute up to $75 (or $150 for couples) and receive a full refund from the State of Minnesota.

Please take advantage of your PCR and make a REFUNDABLE $75 DONATION so Keith can continue fighting to help people afford their lives and live with safety, dignity, and respect.

CONTRIBUTE: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250527ke?refcode=body&amount=25]

name: Golden Portfolio on behalf of Updates For Great Americans
subject: What if America bought gold like it used to?
body:
|
---
Today, gold makes up just 0.5% of U.S. household savings. That’s far below the historical average of 2%. In other words: The public is completely asleep on gold — even though the gold price recently broke through $3,500. If gold demand merely returns to normal the historical average, we’re looking at a 4X surge in gold buying. And that’s before factoring in:
* Central Banks buying gold at record speed...
* Trump unleashing a gold mining renaissance in America...
* [Warren Buffett potentially revealing a massive gold stake by May 15th.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zkq6r7cv3jfj5v8oaj0608d1eh0mssjeqliednv0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmeo74chh2bs)

If that tidal wave of demand hits while supply is already tight... Gold’s price won’t just double. It could go 3X, 4X, even higher — just like it did in the 1970s. And the small miners tied to the best new gold discoveries? They could go up 100X or more. [That’s why I've identified the top four miners for the coming gold mania.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zbjgfsjn9lc53mgm8am98a0gkht449lnb8v5dn20_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmeo74chh2bs) You don’t need to overthink this. You don’t need $1 million in the bank. Even a small $1,000 stake in the right miners — the ones I've already identified — could change your financial future forever. Don't wait until gold is $6,000 or $10,000 an ounce and the public is panicking to buy. By then, it’ll be too late. [Take a position in my top four picks for the coming gold bull market mania.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zu90332tjckqjcpq8ii2o439hqgnf9b35u1q6qto_rp22sh2s8i6eob0spjmeo74chh2bs) This may be the last great gold buying opportunity of our lifetime. Let’s make it count. Garrett Goggin, CFA, CMT
Chief Analyst and Founder, Golden Portfolio

Pop Acta Media | Updates for Great Americans
---

name: Team Imer PAC
subject: Don't wait, WATCH NOW: Our Mission, the future of America depends on all of us!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Team Imer PAC and is not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee
body:
WATCH NOW: Our Mission and objectives here at Team Imer PAC. The future of America depends on all of us...
| | | | | [](https://ghwh6lbbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BVSk8yxFemOezjTS7f6CmSKLtG88eUpeD9e0Jm1H22IGGvUQ0dyn-QUE9xCkG6rHW3iB0xiXHz-tstxfgN_bKOkme94teIfsUl7M3fBEaiwCI-5gxyrG4UqjmEYtm_bEN9UnSoM0f5AIIQqPILQCOZdMllmdyKaqJiid4pgrQCbqvCTGAux-aapmSodsZSSyKKZABdmfJl4=&c=_8R49SUszw0PKm1Yd0uwxgD3mu1d92XhcAEjGuAPckFvSSp0YIac3w==&ch=uJ4d8QhrP_BLyEZqC7qOV98iOadLvGD1QDLRxK3XR8Uy10UZI2L0Iw==)
---
---
| Friend,
When America was founded, it was more than just a nation; it was an idea, an experiment in liberty and self-governance that no one knew would succeed.
Now, as we approach America's 250th Anniversary, we stand united by one common goal: Protecting the American Dream.
America is at a crossroads, and we need your help to protect the American Dream for generations to come.
As we gear up for the 2026 midterm races, we felt it was important to share our mission with you, so we are thrilled to announce the release of our new video, Team Imer PAC: Our Mission.
---
| | [](https://ghwh6lbbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BVSk8yxFemOezjTS7f6CmSKLtG88eUpeD9e0Jm1H22IGGvUQ0dyn-QUE9xCkG6rHW3iB0xiXHz-tstxfgN_bKOkme94teIfsUl7M3fBEaiwCI-5gxyrG4UqjmEYtm_bEN9UnSoM0f5AIIQqPILQCOZdMllmdyKaqJiid4pgrQCbqvCTGAux-aVMM_aIh_w4CgJLuZnYveaw=&c=_8R49SUszw0PKm1Yd0uwxgD3mu1d92XhcAEjGuAPckFvSSp0YIac3w==&ch=uJ4d8QhrP_BLyEZqC7qOV98iOadLvGD1QDLRxK3XR8Uy10UZI2L0Iw==)

| We invite you to watch this short video describing our mission, objectives, and goals here at Team Imer PAC.
However, this mission cannot be completed without support from loyal patriots, like you, who want to see the America we all know and love protected from the radical left, uniparty and the establishment that would rather see it destroyed.
Will you help us secure the future of America for the next generation?

clean_body: WATCH NOW: Our Mission and objectives here at Team Imer PAC. The future of America depends on all of us... | | | | | == | Friend, When America was founded, it was more than just a nation; it was an idea, an experiment in liberty and self-governance that no one knew would succeed. Now, as we approach America's 250th Anniversary, we stand united by one common goal: Protecting the American Dream. America is at a crossroads, and we need your help to protect the American Dream for generations to come. As we gear up for the 2026 midterm races, we felt it was important to share our mission with you, so we are thrilled to announce the release of our new video, Team Imer PAC: Our Mission. | | == | | == | We invite you to watch this short video describing our mission, objectives, and goals here at Team Imer PAC. However, this mission cannot be completed without support from loyal patriots, like you, who want to see the America we all know and love protected from the radical left, uniparty and the establishment that would rather see it destroyed. Will you help us secure the future of America for the next generation? | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | We are committed to this fight, and we will never back down, never give up, and we will fight, fight, fight for the America First agenda! We hope you will watch this video and consider contributing to our mission. What are you waiting for? This is what we're up against right now: a GOP hell-bent on gutting Medicaid and slashing essential programs to reward billionaires and special interests. They want their rich friends to get richer, and they're willing to strip health care and food support from low-income Americans to pay for it.

But EMILYs List women are standing strong and calling it like it is:
"_I'm all about the data, so let me hit you with some numbers: 1 in 3 kids in Oregon are on Medicaid, 80,000 people in our state use SNAP to put food on the table, 13.7 million Americans rely on Medicaid for health care, and 220 Republicans want to steal those benefits. That's BS._ " – **U.S. Rep. Janelle Bynum (OR-05)**

"_Republicans are breaking Medicaid so they can rob it!_ " – **U.S. Rep. Katherine Clark (MA-05)**

"_It is deeply unfortunate, madam chair, that you chose to begin this hearing... literally in the dead of night to try and hide from the American people...My Democratic colleagues and I remain here and continue shedding light on the harms of this big bad bill that only benefits billionaires_." – **U.S. Rep. Emily Randall (WA-06)**
We're raising $150,000 by the end of the month to power EMILYs List candidates like these, who will speak truth to power and unite against Republican robbery from the American people.

This isn't just about holding the line — it's about building momentum. This End-of-Month goal will help elect Democratic pro-choice women who are taking on extremism with honesty, grit, and determination.

[**Will you donate toward our $150,000 goal now to support Democratic pro-choice candidates in the fight?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108450390/547959504/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE567_20250527_EOMText_ALL_v1s4&sourceid=1056218&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0N2UxYWY0NC0xODNiLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EgQ-YlvguB7dgBr-X8ovwS3npXNHC6ZO72Iv4AuVrpA=)

clean_body: This is about building power and protecting millions of Americans. | = Peter, We're already five months into 2025. The attacks from the right haven't let up for a second — but neither have we. That's why we're launching our $150,000 End-of-Month fund. = | = This is what we're up against right now: a GOP hell-bent on gutting Medicaid and slashing essential programs to reward billionaires and special interests. They want their rich friends to get richer, and they're willing to strip health care and food support from low-income Americans to pay for it. But EMILYs List women are standing strong and calling it like it is: "I'm all about the data, so let me hit you with some numbers: 1 in 3 kids in Oregon are on Medicaid, 80,000 people in our state use SNAP to put food on the table, 13.7 million Americans rely on Medicaid for health care, and 220 Republicans want to steal those benefits. That's BS. " – U.S. Rep. Janelle Bynum (OR-05) "Republicans are breaking Medicaid so they can rob it! " – U.S. Rep. Katherine Clark (MA-05) "It is deeply unfortunate, madam chair, that you chose to begin this hearing... literally in the dead of night to try and hide from the American people...My Democratic colleagues and I remain here and continue shedding light on the harms of this big bad bill that only benefits billionaires." – U.S. Rep. Emily Randall (WA-06) We're raising $150,000 by the end of the month to power EMILYs List candidates like these, who will speak truth to power and unite against Republican robbery from the American people. This isn't just about holding the line — it's about building momentum. This End-of-Month goal will help elect Democratic pro-choice women who are taking on extremism with honesty, grit, and determination. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, the EMILYs List Campaigns team = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. ## This week on The Sean Spicer Show...
Tonight John Solomon of the Just the News joins us -- tomorrow Chicks on the Right will be with us to break down the latest with President Trump's fight with Harvard. On Thursday I'll have a great panel featuring David Avella of GOPACE, Ashley Hayek from America First Policy Institute, and Stephen Gardner will make his show debut. And don't miss Friday, when I'll be joined by President Trump pollsters -- BOTH Jim and John McLaughlin will share insights on new polling.
## Winner or washout?
There's been talk for months of Trump's "big, beautiful" budget bill.
It's been the source of a lot of controversy -- even in-fighting within the Republican party and members of Congress on the right.
Some complain it doesn't cut enough spending, others argue it doesn't goes to far or doesn't contain more a certain tax provision.
Well Thursday, the big bill passed its first big hurdle: it narrowly made it through the House after plenty of deal-making and good old-fashioned politics.
This is a big win for Trump's agenda, but it's only step one.
Now it has to make it through the Senate -- which definitely won't be a cakewalk.
While the House has a narrow 4 seat majority with competing interests, the Senate Republicans hold a 3 seat majority with almost equal factions of warring parties.
Especially since Sen. Ron Johnson and a handful of other senators are determined to stop the bill from moving forward without more significant spending cuts or reductions in deficit spending.
Johnson even compared the current bill as written to [the Titanic](https://substack.com/redirect/0e6a95da-3ebc-4dc5-b181-cfaef8579052?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Does he have a point?...

clean_body: Let's talk about the "big, beautiful bill" | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Let's talk about the "big, beautiful bill" | | | May 27| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | ICYMI: Trump's rising SUPERSTARS... Stories I'm reading... \- via Politico \- via Semafor \- via New York Times \- via The Independent \- via Wall Street Journal \- via Axios This week on The Sean Spicer Show... Tonight John Solomon of the Just the News joins us -- tomorrow Chicks on the Right will be with us to break down the latest with President Trump's fight with Harvard. On Thursday I'll have a great panel featuring David Avella of GOPACE, Ashley Hayek from America First Policy Institute, and Stephen Gardner will make his show debut. And don't miss Friday, when I'll be joined by President Trump pollsters -- BOTH Jim and John McLaughlin will share insights on new polling. Winner or washout? There's been talk for months of Trump's "big, beautiful" budget bill. It's been the source of a lot of controversy -- even in-fighting within the Republican party and members of Congress on the right. Some complain it doesn't cut enough spending, others argue it doesn't goes to far or doesn't contain more a certain tax provision. Well Thursday, the big bill passed its first big hurdle: it narrowly made it through the House after plenty of deal-making and good old-fashioned politics. This is a big win for Trump's agenda, but it's only step one. Now it has to make it through the Senate -- which definitely won't be a cakewalk. While the House has a narrow 4 seat majority with competing interests, the Senate Republicans hold a 3 seat majority with almost equal factions of warring parties. Especially since Sen. Ron Johnson and a handful of other senators are determined to stop the bill from moving forward without more significant spending cuts or reductions in deficit spending. Johnson even compared the current bill as written to . Does he have a point?... | **Meet Jim:** Congressman Jim Baird is a decorated Vietnam War Veteran and the proud representative for Indiana's 4th Congressional District in our nation's capital. A lifelong resident of West Central Indiana, Jim grew up on his family's farm and had the values of faith, family, and hard work instilled in him at an early age. He is an unwavering defender of America's Constitutional liberties and has spent the vast majority of his life serving our country. After earning both his bachelor's and his master's at Purdue University, Jim was assigned to the 523rd Transportation Company at Cha Rang Valley in Vietnam. He fought in two of the deadliest battles of Operation Lam Son 719 and lost his left arm during a convoy ambush in March of 1971. In a true embodiment of American sacrifice and patriotism, Jim Baird has put his life on the line time and time again fighting communism abroad. His belief in the American Dream and our American way of life go far beyond words. For the entirety of his life, Jim has fought the good fight for our country both physically and spiritually. And he wears his love for the world's greatest country on his sleeve each and every day that he shows up for work in Washington, D.C. Previously, Jim Baird spent eight years in the Indiana House of Representatives serving District 44 from 2010 to 2018. Prior to that, he was Putnam County Commissioner from 2006 to 2010. A fiscal conservative and America First Republican, Jim is incredibly proud to fight every day in DC to advance the best interests of Indiana's families through Congress. A loving husband to his wife Danise, a devoted father to his children, and the world's best pop-pop to his grandkids, Jim spends most of his free time in the company of his loved ones or tending to the family farm.

For nearly all the time I've been on this Earth, all I've wanted to do is serve the country that has given everything to me. So when it comes to my service in Washington, it's the people's best interests that dictate every single decision I make on Capitol Hill. I can't thank you enough for your continued support in the good fight to restore glory to the world's greatest country in 2025 and beyond. | | | If you're NOT in a position to donate, please refrain from doing so. I would never want you to cause financial strain for yourself or your family. There are a multitude of other ways you can contribute to the fight for IN-04 and the American people. Please click the link below and sign up to VOLUNTEER for Team Baird today! | | | OUR STORE IS OPEN! We have great deals running all year long on official Team Baird merch. Shop today and don't apologize for being loud and proud in your love for our great country! | | | | | | If you would like to donate by mail, please write the check out to: Committee to Elect Jim Baird PO Box 203, Greencastle, IN 46135 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C). All rights reserved. | |

name: Chris Murphy
subject: personally asking
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
The minute they perceive us to be weak is the minute they will pounce.
#

[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=1&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)
---
Friends, it’s Senator Chris Murphy.
I was proud to help lead the fight in 2022 for the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act -- the most important national gun law in decades. **But it was your efforts as part of this grassroots movement alongside GIFFORDS that got this bill across the line and signed into law.**
I saw this firsthand. Republicans were forced to realize that our movement was -- and remains -- more powerful than the gun lobby.
This week marks the third anniversary of the horrific tragedy in Uvalde. During those 30 days between the shooting and the passage of the law, our movement owned the media space, shaped the debate in Washington, and dominated the phone lines.
YOU made this happen -- and that is how we passed a law that has already saved countless lives.
**Today, we must apply these same lessons to our fight to stop the Trump administration from rolling back our hard-fought progress.**
In President Trump’s first months in office, he’s shown he cares more about the corporate gun industry’s profits than our communities’ safety.
He disbanded the White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention. He ordered a review of the gun safety measures we fought for and won during the Biden-Harris administration — including overwhelmingly popular actions that expanded background checks. And every day, his administration is pulling every lever they can to enrich their gun industry allies.
Our job is to flood the zone just like they’re doing — to be there every single day to rebut their lies, correct the record, and remind Americans why the daily epidemic of gun violence is happening.
**And we must put the White House and the gun lobby on the defensive about why they want more families to experience the trauma of gun violence firsthand.**
We have to be in this fight every single day. A day off is a day we’re losing. I am not taking a day off in our efforts to fight back against Trump and the gun lobby, and I hope you won’t either. Because GIFFORDS needs you now more than ever.
[**If you can, please make a contribution to support GIFFORDS PAC’s efforts to fight for a world free from gun violence. Whatever you can spare will help them hold the gun industry accountable.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=2&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-may25.u1Lybj&amount=3&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=21&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://go.giffords.org/go/17017?c0=giffords-em-may25.yeliDC&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=22&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://go.giffords.org/go/17010?c0=giffords-em-may25.6Aau9J&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=23&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://go.giffords.org/go/17011?c0=giffords-em-may25.hX5Ig4&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=24&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://go.giffords.org/go/17012?c0=giffords-em-may25.pzzzTg&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=25&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-may25.u1Lybj&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=26&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
The reality is this: While the Trump administration wants to roll back gun safety laws and re-open loopholes, they haven’t acted as quickly and aggressively as they have on other issues because they recognize that our movement is strong.
The minute they perceive us to be weak or think that we’re asleep at the wheel is the minute they will pounce.
Let’s not give them that chance.
Instead, let’s spend every day reminding them that we’re here and stronger than ever. Our power does not come from Washington. It comes from you and the millions of people like you who are in this fight.
We have the American people on our side. And as long as we show the strength we have -- and our willingness to use it -- we’ll have the upper hand.
**GIFFORDS is made for this moment with a plan to take on the Trump Administration and build bipartisan support for commonsense measures to reduce gun violence.**
[**Please donate to GIFFORDS PAC today. This is a critical moment. Take it from someone who sees your impact on the floor of the United States Senate — your activism and generosity make all the difference in the world.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=27&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-may25.u1Lybj&amount=3&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=46&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://go.giffords.org/go/17017?c0=giffords-em-may25.yeliDC&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=47&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://go.giffords.org/go/17010?c0=giffords-em-may25.6Aau9J&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=48&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://go.giffords.org/go/17011?c0=giffords-em-may25.hX5Ig4&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=49&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://go.giffords.org/go/17012?c0=giffords-em-may25.pzzzTg&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=50&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-may25.u1Lybj&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=51&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**
---
****
Thank you for all you do. I am so proud that we’re in this together.
Every best wish,
Chris Murphy
United States Senator
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250527-1-b&t=52&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=53&akid=10471%2E3935704%2EpI08nk) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

“The job of the U.S. Armed Forces is not to host drag shows, to
transform foreign cultures, but to spread democracy to everybody
around the world at the point of a gun. The military’s job is to
dominate any foe and annihilate any threat to America, anywhere,

[image] [] RIGHTS AND FREEDOMS IN PERIL "When it comes to fighting for the American people’s ‘right to know,’ no one holds a candle to Tom Fitton and his team at Judicial Watch" - SEAN HANNITY Tom Fitton returns with an exhaustive investigation into the progressive movement’s efforts to dismantle the venerable institutions of American rights and freedoms. Order Tom Fitton's Must-Read Today! [] [600x300] [] ‘This is the Golden Age’: Trump delivers commencement speech to West Point graduates [] Source: The Hill [] “The job of the U.S. Armed Forces is not to host drag shows, to transform foreign cultures, but to spread democracy to everybody around the world at the point of a gun. The military’s job is to dominate any foe and annihilate any threat to America, anywhere, anytime and any place,” President Trump said.
